data.title: Amazon (AMZN) Launches New AI Healthcare Assistant
data.description:
E-commerce giant Amazon (AMZN) has launched a new artificial intelligence (AI) healthcare assistant for members of its primary care chain called One Medical.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Amazon joins competitors OpenAI and Anthropic, each of which recently launched similar AI healthcare assistants. Amazon's new tool, called Health AI, uses large language models to answer people's health questions and provide them with advice based on their medical records, lab results, and medications.

Health AI can also assist with managing people's medications and book medical appointments for them with a One Medical provider. Health AI is available to people through the One Medical app. Amazon acquired One Medical for $3.9 billion in 2023. One Medical runs brick-and-mortar medical clinics and offers telehealth services to members who pay $99 to $199 a year for its services.

The Limits of Amazon's Health AI

Health AI has some limits. Amazon says it isn't intended to provide diagnosis or treatment for people, and it shouldn't replace a doctor's visit or trip to an Emergency Room in the event of a serious medical incident. The AI tool is mostly intended to identify symptoms and book in-person doctor visits for users.

Competitors of Amazon are also rolling out AI healthcare tools. Earlier in January, privately held OpenAI launched ChatGPT Health, a tool that enables people to upload medical records and receive personalized advice. Another AI startup, Anthropic, has released Claude for Healthcare, which helps people with healthcare issues.

Is AMZN Stock a Buy?

AMZN stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 47 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 46 Buy and one Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average AMZN price target of $294.93 implies 27.34% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Rally as Trump Withdraws Greenland Tariffs, Says Force Won't be Used in Acquisition Deal
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed higher on Wednesday after President Trump pulled back tariffs on eight European countries and announced a framework deal that wouldn't involve using military force to purchase Greenland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Alongside NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, Trump has formed a framework deal to acquire Greenland. Earlier in the day, he ruled out using force to take over the self-governing territory, easing geopolitical tensions.

"Based upon this understanding, I will not be imposing the Tariffs that were scheduled to go into effect on February 1st," Trump said in a Truth Social post. "Additional discussions are being held concerning The Golden Dome as it pertains to Greenland." Trump didn't specify the terms of the deal, although he told CNBC that it would be permanent with the details to be publicized soon. He added that the negotiations would be led by Vice President JD Vance, Secretary of State Marco Rubio, Special Envoy Steve Witkoff, and "various others."

Trump had previously stated that the tariffs would remain in effect until the U.S. reached a deal to purchase Greenland. Danish Foreign Minister Lars Løkke Rasmussen acknowledged the withdrawal of the tariffs as a positive sign, although he reiterated that Denmark wouldn't be able to accommodate Trump's requests.

Several financial leaders are in attendance at the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in Davos, Switzerland, including JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon and Citadel CEO and founder Ken Griffin. During a panel interview, Dimon warned that Trump's proposed credit card interest rate limit would be an "economic disaster" for Americans. "In the worst case, you'd have to have a drastic reduction of the credit card business," Dimon said. "You would remove credit from 80% of Americans and that is their backup credit."

Meanwhile, Griffin stressed that "recent research" indicates that the Trump administration's tariffs have "come at the expense of the American people." A study published this week by the Kiel Institute, a German research firm, concluded that Americans have paid for 96% of the tariffs, while foreign exporters have absorbed just 4%.

Looking ahead, the Bureau of Economic Analysis will release its second estimate of gross domestic product (GDP) for the third quarter on Thursday, while the Department of Labor will publish initial and continuing jobless claims.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 1.16% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 1.36%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Arista Networks (ANET) Traders Eye Breakout Following Stock Implosion
data.description:
Arista Networks (ANET) has struggled since early November, making exposure to ANET stock a higher-risk gamble. At the same time, it's fair to point out that the company offers a relevant business. As a developer and marketer of cloud networking solutions, Arista is likely to benefit from stronger tailwinds, such as the data center hardware refresh cycle. Subsequently, ANET's value compression may present an attractive opportunity for contrarians.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fundamentally, Arista's bullish case mostly centers on substantive metrics, particularly robust revenue growth. Indeed, the top line has accelerated due to rising demand in the artificial intelligence, cloud, and enterprise sectors, indicating an ideal market positioning. Further, the company enjoys financial flexibility thanks to its debt-free balance sheet and high return on equity. Finally, its acquisition of VeloCloud should bolster Arista's enterprise offerings, allowing it to expand its overall influence.
data.author: Joshua Enomoto

data.title: 'Meaningful Step Forward': Oklo Stock Jumps as BofA Goes Bullish on Meta Deal
data.description:
Oklo's (OKLO) shares climbed about 5% early Wednesday after Bank of America (BofA) (BAC) turned bullish on the stock, calling the nuclear startup's new deal with Meta (META) a "meaningful step forward."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Earlier this month, Oklo ignited hope in its advanced nuclear technology after it disclosed that it has secured a long-term 1.2 gigawatt supply contract from Meta. The tech supermajor also tapped two other companies for nuclear energy; they are Vistra Energy (VST) and billionaire Bill Gates-backed TerraPower.

BofA Upbeat after Oklo's 'Modest' Deal with Meta

Responding to the update, BofA analyst Dimple Gosai, who was previously bearish Oklo due to investor concern about the startup's capital expenditure and the timing of its power purchase agreements with clients — upgraded Oklo from Hold to Buy.

Gosai raised her OKLO price target by 14% from $111 per share to $127, implying more than 41% upside. The analyst noted that while the capacity to be supplied is "modest," the deal marks a "meaningful step forward" and offers concrete evidence of Oklo's execution and its partner's commitment.

Furthermore, the BofA analyst noted that Meta's decision to invest capital years before the project comes online highlights how critical nuclear power has become as AI and data center energy needs ramp up.

Oklo's Losses Widen

The analyst's upbeat comment comes even as Oklo in its recent third-quarter results released in November reported a worse-than-expected net loss. Gosai previously said Oklo has made "constructive" progress by securing regulatory approvals, thereby reducing the risks associated with its fuel source.

Oklo, which remains unprofitable, broke ground on its first Aurora nuclear reactor plant in late September. The startup has said it is working to deliver its first nuclear power plant by 2028.

Is Oklo a Good Stock to Buy?

Across Wall Street, analysts' consensus rating for Oklo remains a Moderate Buy. This is based on eight Buys and four Holds issued by 12 analysts over the past three months.

However, the average OKLO price target of $126.89 implies about 41% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Nvidia, Palantir and Dell Stocks Rise on Deal to Build AI Data Centers across EMEA
data.description:
Shares in tech firms Nvidia (NVDA), Palantir (PLTR) and Dell (DELL) edged higher early Wednesday after UK-based Sovereign AI picked them and professional services firm Accenture (ACN) to build AI data centers across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa (EMEA).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Palantir and Accenture Spearhead Sovereign Cloud Project

The partnership is being spearheaded by Palantir, which will lead the software front while Accenture helps with operational control, including sales and engineering support. Specifically, Palantir will use its Chain Reaction operating system to build and deploy the AI infrastructure.

To achieve this, the Dell AI Factory with Nvidia — Dell's end-to-end stack of hardware, software and services — will function as the "secure, high-performance backbone" for the project. The stack is powered by Nvidia's AI capabilities such as the Blackwell graphics processing unit and is specifically tailored for massive AI workloads

Sovereign AI noted that the goal is to "deliver a resilient sovereign AI foundation for commercial and government sectors" across EMEA.

The Sovereign Cloud Race Heats Up

Sovereign AI refers to a type of cloud infrastructure that maintains data within a specific country or region in terms of physical location, legal frameworks, and management. 

"This collaboration with Accenture and Palantir will help accelerate the development and deployment of [Palantir's] Dell AI Factory- and NVIDIA-powered sovereign-grade AI capabilities, supporting economic security and digital resiliency across EMEA, with plans to expand into Asia Pacific," Sovereign AI explained in a statement.

The deal builds on an existing partnership between Palantir and Accenture, which together formed the Accenture Palantir Business Group in December. Palantir named Accenture its global partner for enterprise transformation as part of the deal.

Meanwhile, the new project comes as governments and industries across the world continue to consider sovereign AI as critical. Last week, Amazon Web Services (AMZN) launched the European Sovereign Cloud in Germany as its first sovereign cloud service.

data.title: Netflix Stock (NFLX) Faces Divided Views from Morgan Stanley and Goldman After Q4 Results
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) stock remains in focus after its latest earnings report, which showed steady growth but also raised new questions about higher spending plans, the Warner Bros. deal, and valuation. Following the release, some analysts remain bullish on Netflix's long-term earnings power, while others are urging patience as investors wait for more clarity.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Q4 Earnings Snapshot

Netflix reported a solid quarter, with revenue, operating profit, and free cash flow all coming in strong. The company reported fourth-quarter revenue of $12.1 billion, modestly above Wall Street expectations of $11.97 billion, while adjusted earnings came in at $0.56 per share, also slightly ahead of forecasts of $0.55 per share.

Meanwhile, the company also said advertising revenue more than doubled last year and is expected to grow further in 2026. However, Netflix confirmed it has paused share buybacks as it prepares for its proposed all-cash deal for Warner Bros. Discovery's (WBD) streaming and studio assets.

Bullish View: Morgan Stanley Sees Long-Term EPS Growth

Morgan Stanley analyst Benjamin Swinburne kept an Overweight rating on Netflix but lowered his price target to $110 from $120. Swinburne said his positive view is based on Netflix's ability to grow adjusted earnings by more than 20% per year through 2028, driven by scale, improving margins, and strong returns on content spending. He also highlighted Netflix's fast-growing ad business, which grew more than 100% year over year in 2025 and is expected to reach over $3 billion in revenue in 2026.

On the Warner Bros deal, Swinburne said risks appear largely priced in, noting that Netflix's forward valuation has already come down sharply. He added that if the deal closes, it could improve earnings and boost returns on content over time.

Cautious View: Goldman Sachs Wants More Clarity

Goldman Sachs analyst Eric Sheridan lowered his price target to $100 from $112 and kept a Neutral rating.

Sheridan acknowledged Netflix's strong Q4 results and said the company could still deliver solid revenue growth and improving margins in 2026. However, he said investors may hold back until there is more clarity on the cost, structure, and impact of the WBD deal.

He added that while the deal could help Netflix grow and compete, the stock may stay under pressure until investors have a clearer view on long-term returns and how cash will be used.

Is NFLX Stock a Good Buy Now?  

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on NFLX stock based on 26 Buys, nine Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average NFLX price target of $124.63 per share implies 43% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Trump Cancels Greenland Tariffs Following 'Framework' Acquisition Plan
data.description:
President Trump announced on Wednesday that he will not move forward with his original proposal to impose a 10% tariff on eight European countries. The decision followed talks with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, which led to a "framework of a future deal" to acquire the self-governing territory.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"Based upon this understanding, I will not be imposing the Tariffs that were scheduled to go into effect on February 1st," Trump said in a Truth Social post. "Additional discussions are being held concerning The Golden Dome as it pertains to Greenland."

Denmark Pushes Back as Trump Seeks Greenland Talks

Trump didn't immediately specify the details of the deal, although he mentioned that negotiations would be led by Vice President JD Vance, Secretary of State Marco Rubio, and Special Envoy Steve Witkoff.

Earlier today, Trump called for immediate negotiations to acquire Greenland and ruled out using force as part of his plan. Danish Foreign Minister Lars Løkke Rasmussen rejected Trump's request for a discussion but said it was a "positive sign" that Trump will avoid utilizing the U.S. military.

data.title: Tesla, BYD Face Memory Chip Shortage in 2026, Wells Fargo Warns
data.description:
Analysts at Wells Fargo, one of Wall Street's leading research firms, released a note stating that automakers could face renewed cost pressures and potential supply interruptions as a widening global memory-chip shortage intensifies, spurred by surging demand from data centers and AI workloads.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The firm noted that the top 10 automotive Tier 1 suppliers consumed about 54% of total DRAM (Dynamic Random Access Memory) in 2025, led by giants like BYD (BYDDF), Tesla (TSLA), and Aptiv (APTV). Yet autos account for under 10% of the global DRAM market, leaving the sector at a disadvantage as chipmakers prioritize higher-margin clients in cloud computing and AI.

Tight Parts Supply Could Raise Auto Prices

DRAM is widely used for vehicle infotainment, advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS), and centralized electronic architectures. Its prices have skyrocketed lately, stripping carmakers of bargaining leverage. Wells Fargo noted that DDR5 spot prices have surged over eight times and DDR4 more than sixteen times above 2024 averages.

Analysts estimate current DRAM content at $50 to $110 per vehicle, cautioning that these hikes could deliver a hefty cost hit to the auto sector in 2026. They noted that global DRAM demand is expected to climb by about 26% next year, outpacing supply growth of roughly 21%.

Micron (MU) remains the largest supplier to the auto sector, along with Samsung (SSNLF) and SK Hynix. These chipmakers are allocating greater time and resources to build the faster growing and more profitable data center markets than the auto sector.

Consequently, automakers and suppliers may face steeper prices to secure supply, a risk Wells Fargo warns could squeeze margins and disrupt production schedules. Analysts added that premium vehicle segments and EV makers appear most vulnerable due to their elevated memory usage per vehicle.

Wells Fargo already sees emerging signs of panic buying similar to that of the 2021 chip crisis, warning that failure to absorb higher costs could trigger production halts.

Tesla or BYD: Which Is the Better Auto Stock?

A tightening memory market could raise auto costs and pressure margins, particularly for memory-heavy EVs like Tesla and BYD.

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to determine which stock between Tesla and BYD is currently favored by analysts. Notably, BYDDF stock has a "Strong Buy," while Tesla has a "Hold" consensus, suggesting analysts remain highly optimistic about BYD's long-term prospects.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Snowflake's (SNOW) Growth Spurt is 'Only Just Getting Started,' Says Analyst
data.description:
Snowflake (SNOW), a cloud-based data platform headquartered in Montana, surged out of the gate following its 2020 IPO but has encountered several headwinds more recently. The stock is down about 25% from its 52-week high of just over $280 last November and is trading nearly 50% below its 2021 peak, when data platform names were enjoying a strong run.

data.title: Oklo Stock Rises as Meta Deal Triggers Bank of America Upgrade
data.description:
Shares of Oklo (OKLO) closed higher in Wednesday's trading after Bank of America (BAC) upgraded the stock from Hold to Buy. The bank also raised its price target from $111 to $127 due to Oklo's newly signed agreement with tech giant Meta (META) to develop a phased 1.2-gigawatt advanced nuclear campus. The deal is seen as an important step forward for the small modular reactor developer as it moves closer to real-world deployment.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Importantly, the agreement includes customer prepayments that help fund early development before final power purchase agreements are signed. While the initial megawatt contribution is relatively small, Bank of America analyst Dimple Gosai said that it offers clear proof of execution and customer commitment. In his view, the Meta deal shows that advanced nuclear projects are beginning to see tangible progress. In addition, Gosai said that Oklo remains his preferred way to gain exposure to nuclear power tied to data center demand.

He noted that Meta's willingness to commit capital years ahead of delivery, even before interconnections or licenses, highlights just how critical nuclear energy could become as AI and data center power needs grow. Gosai also downplayed worries that incremental geothermal supply from data center operator Switch could crowd out Oklo by arguing that those projects are small and do not meaningfully reduce the roughly 12 gigawatts of potential capacity under Oklo's existing agreement with Switch.

Is OKLO Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on OKLO stock based on eight Buys, four Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average OKLO price target of $126.89 per share implies 39.8% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
